hypothesis: If the Segment Anything Model 2 is fully fine-tuned with box prompts on a physics-informed synthetic LPTEM video dataset spanning liquid thickness–controlled SNR, particle counts, and resolutions, then the resulting SAM-EM will segment and track nanoparticles in LPTEM videos more accurately (higher Jaccard, F1, and J&F) than a U-Net baseline and yield centroid positions that agree with manual annotations on real data, because foundation-model priors combined with task-specific synthetic training confer robustness to low SNR, overlaps, and domain shift.
